## CONTEXT ROADMAP
(Index of what the context contains and where to find it)

[cr-00029] PhD thesis (~555k): Zamyshlyaev (~24k-26k), eq mass (~100k-110k), valid (~102k-108k). Benson: hourglass (24k-28k), projection (38.5k-39.6k), ALE condition (Sec1.6.4.1). Oezarmut: hourglass (~10.3k-10.6k). Gravity dam: near-field UNDEX Sec2.3.

[cr-00053] Benson paper: Sec1.6.4.1 defines DeBar's consistency condition (~107000-108200) – the only location in context defining the term. Also: advection req (~89500-90800); Sec1.3 operator splitting; Sec1.4.2 central difference; Sec1.4.7.2 mean stress mixture theory; Sec1.6.5.2 Level Set method for fluid interface tracking.

## CONTEXT UNDERSTANDING
(High-level understanding of the context: what it is, how it's organized, and what matters)

[cu-00033] Hourglass modes (零能量变形模式): zero-energy deformation in reduced-integration shell elements. Benson: 2D quad → 2 modes; 3D hex → 12 modes. Control: assumed strain, hourglass control (Oezarmut). In UNDEX simulations, hourglass modes can contaminate strain fields, shifting the location of max equivalent strain away from the physical position — explains offset strain localization in cylindrical shells.

[cu-00047] Benson paper: three basic requirements for advection (transport) algorithms: (1) Conservation – integrals of transported variables (mass, momentum, energy) preserved; (2) Monotonicity – no new extrema or oscillations; (3) Minimum second-order accuracy – to avoid excessive numerical diffusion.

[cu-00049] Benson paper (Sec 1.3) describes operator splitting for ALE: The governing equations are split into Lagrangian step (∂φ/∂t = S(x,t), grid moves with material, w=0) and Eulerian step (∂φ/∂t = −w·∂φ/∂x, remap variables to new grid). Splitting is accurate to second order; if Eulerian step is treated as projection onto a grid that exactly represents the old solution, accuracy can exceed second order.

[cu-00054] Benson Sec1.4.7.2: '平均应力混合理论' (Mean stress mixture theory) for multi-material elements with different stiffnesses. Assigns strain rates to equalize stress across materials; soft materials compress first, hard materials not over-compressed. Implementation: one Newton iteration per time step for gradual equilibration.

[cu-00056] Consistency condition (DeBar): constraint ensuring uniform velocity field stays uniform after transport; requires ΔM^(i+1/2) = (ΔM^i + ΔM^(i+1))/2. Defined only in Benson Sec1.6.4.1.

[cu-00057] Level Set method (Benson 1.6.5.2): interface represented by zero of signed distance φ; update via Hamilton-Jacobi ∂φ/∂t+v·∇φ=0 using upwind differencing & RK; periodic reinitialization. Advantages: natural topological changes, easy normals/curvature. Disadvantages: volume loss, higher cost than VOF.

## DOMAIN CONSTANTS
(Exact parameters, formulas, thresholds, reference values, enum sets, and output field requirements defined by the context.)

[dc-00040] Grüneisen EOS parameter C values found in context:
- Oezarmut (Table2): C = 1435 m/s
- 基于LS-DYNA的液电效应冲击波数值模拟: c_s = 1484 m/s
- 水下爆炸冲击荷载作用下混凝土重力坝的破坏模式: c = 1.647 km/s
- 不同加筋结构在水中接触爆炸下的破损规律: defines C as '介质声速' (no explicit numeric value)
Note: C is always the speed of sound at zero pressure.

[dc-00052] Central difference method (Benson Sec 1.4.2): second-order accurate; truncation error proportional to (Δt)^3.
